questions.question: Which element is used to group related options within a <select> menu?
questions.explanation: The <optgroup> element groups related <option> elements under a label within a dropdown. It uses the label attribute to display the group name.

questions.question: What does the multiple attribute on a <select> element allow?
choices: Multiple <select> elements on the same page
The user to select more than one option at a time
Multiple forms to share the same dropdown
The dropdown to appear in multiple locations
questions.explanation: The multiple attribute changes the select control from a single-choice dropdown to a list box where the user can select multiple options, typically by holding Ctrl or Cmd while clicking.

questions.question: Which element provides a list of predefined suggestions for an <input> field without restricting the user's input?
questions.explanation: The <datalist> element provides autocomplete suggestions for an input field. Unlike <select>, the user can still type a custom value that is not in the list.

questions.question: Which attribute on an <option> element makes it chosen by default when the page loads?
questions.explanation: The selected attribute marks an option as the default selection. When the form loads, this option will be pre-selected in the dropdown.

questions.question: The ___ attribute on a <select> element controls how many options are visible at once without scrolling.
questions.explanation: The size attribute specifies the number of visible options in the list. When size is greater than 1, the select renders as a list box instead of a dropdown.

questions.question: An <input> element is linked to a <datalist> by setting the input's ___ attribute to the datalist's id.
questions.explanation: The list attribute on an <input> element references the id of a <datalist>, connecting the input to its predefined suggestions.
